title: callitriche palustris subsp. hamulata
intro: Callitriche palustris subsp. hamulata, also known as marsh starwort, is a subspecies of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Plantaginaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating urinary tract infections and kidney stones due to its diuretic and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Callitriche palustris subsp. hamulata include reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms associated with kidney and urinary issues, such as pain and discomfort. Herbal preparations of the plant often involve drying and processing the leaves and stems, which can be made into teas, infusions, or capsules for medicinal use.
